name: Array Bounds Check
description: Verify that array accesses are within bounds [0, len(arr))
formal_spec: PRE: arr has length n > 0; POST: all array accesses use indices in [0, n-1]

name: No Null Dereference
description: Verify that pointers/references are not null before dereferencing
formal_spec: PRE: ptr is not null; POST: dereference is safe

name: SQL Injection Prevention
description: Verify that SQL queries use parameterized queries, not string concatenation
formal_spec: PRE: user_input is untrusted; POST: query uses parameterized statement

name: Array Invariant
description: Verify array properties maintained throughout algorithm
formal_spec: PRE: array is sorted; INV: array remains sorted; POST: array is still sorted
test_code:
def maintains_sorted(arr):
    assert all(arr[i] <= arr[i+1] for i in range(len(arr)-1))

name: Return Value Correctness
description: Verify that return values match postconditions
formal_spec: PRE: function signature; POST: returned value satisfies postcondition
test_code:
def validate_return(result, expected):
    assert result == expected or result == -1

name: Loop Termination
description: Verify that loops terminate after finite iterations
formal_spec: PRE: loop has bounded counter; POST: loop terminates

name: Recursion Base Case
description: Verify that recursive functions have a base case
formal_spec: PRE: function is recursive; POST: has base case that prevents infinite recursion

name: Array Mutation Safety
description: Verify that array mutations preserve invariants
formal_spec: PRE: array has property P; POST: after mutation, array still has property P
test_code:
def safe_mutation(arr):
    original_len = len(arr)
    arr[0] = 99
    assert len(arr) == original_len

name: XSS Prevention
description: Verify that user-controlled output is escaped before rendering in HTML context

name: Path Traversal Prevention
description: Verify that file paths built from user input cannot escape the intended directory
test_code:
import os
def safe_path(base_dir, user_path):
    full = os.path.realpath(os.path.join(base_dir, user_path))
    assert full.startswith(os.path.realpath(base_dir))
    return full

name: Command Injection Prevention
description: Verify that shell commands do not interpolate unsanitized user input

name: Insecure Deserialization Prevention
description: Verify that untrusted data is not deserialized with unsafe loaders (e.g. pickle)

name: No Hardcoded Secrets
description: Verify that credentials, API keys, and tokens are not hardcoded in source
test_code:
import os
api_key = os.environ['API_KEY']

name: No Weak Cryptography
description: Verify that weak hash/cipher algorithms (MD5, SHA1, DES) are not used for security purposes

name: XXE Prevention
description: Verify that XML parsers disable external entity resolution

name: SSRF Prevention
description: Verify that outbound requests to user-supplied URLs validate against an allowlist
test_code:
def safe_fetch(url, allowlist):
    from urllib.parse import urlparse
    host = urlparse(url).hostname
    assert host in allowlist

name: CSRF Token Required
description: Verify that state-changing endpoints require a valid CSRF token
test_code:
def handle_post(request):
    assert request.csrf_token == session.csrf_token

name: No Unsafe eval/exec
description: Verify that eval()/exec() are not called on user-controlled strings
test_code:
def safe_compute(expression_ast):
    return evaluate_ast(expression_ast)  # not raw eval()

name: Off-by-One Prevention
description: Verify loop and index bounds do not systematically over- or under-shoot by one
test_code:
for i in range(len(arr)):
    process(arr[i])

name: Integer Overflow Prevention
description: Verify arithmetic on bounded integer types cannot silently wrap around
test_code: assert result <= INT_MAX and result >= INT_MIN

name: No Unsafe Type Coercion
description: Verify implicit type conversions do not lose precision or change semantics silently
test_code: value = int(user_input) if user_input.isdigit() else None

name: Division by Zero Prevention
description: Verify divisor is checked to be non-zero before division

name: No Uninitialized Variable Use
description: Verify all variables are assigned before being read

name: No Mutable Default Arguments
description: Verify function defaults do not use mutable objects (list, dict) shared across calls

name: No Direct Float Equality
description: Verify floating point values are compared with a tolerance, not exact equality
test_code: assert abs(a - b) < 1e-9

name: Unicode Normalization Before Comparison
description: Verify strings from external sources are normalized before equality comparison
test_code:
import unicodedata
a = unicodedata.normalize('NFC', s1)
b = unicodedata.normalize('NFC', s2)
assert a == b

name: No Silent Exception Swallowing
description: Verify caught exceptions are logged or re-raised, not silently discarded

name: Bounded Recursion Depth
description: Verify recursive calls strictly decrease toward the base case

name: Consistent Return Types
description: Verify a function returns the same type across all code paths

name: No Unprotected Shared Mutable State
description: Verify shared mutable state accessed by multiple threads is protected by a lock
test_code:
with lock:
    counter += 1

name: Consistent Lock Ordering
description: Verify multiple locks are always acquired in the same global order to prevent deadlock
test_code:
with lock_a:
    with lock_b:
        critical_section()

name: Atomic Counter Updates
description: Verify counters shared across threads use atomic operations instead of read-modify-write
test_code:
from threading import Lock
counter_lock = Lock()
def increment():
    with counter_lock:
        counter[0] += 1

name: Safe Double-Checked Locking
description: Verify double-checked locking patterns use proper memory barriers/volatile semantics

name: No Check-Then-Act Race
description: Verify check-then-act sequences on shared resources are performed atomically

name: Bounded Thread/Worker Pool
description: Verify concurrent worker pools have an upper bound to prevent resource exhaustion
test_code:
from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=10)

name: Avoid Unnecessary Quadratic Nested Loops
description: Verify nested loops over the same collection are replaced with a hash-based lookup when possible
test_code:
seen = set(other_list)
result = [x for x in items if x in seen]

name: Avoid String Concatenation in Loop
description: Verify strings are built with a list/join or buffer instead of repeated += in a loop
test_code:
parts = []
for item in items:
    parts.append(str(item))
result = ''.join(parts)

name: Prefer Generators for Large Sequences
description: Verify large intermediate sequences use generators/iterators instead of materialized lists

name: Memoize Repeated Pure Computation
description: Verify expensive pure functions called repeatedly with the same arguments are memoized

name: Use Appropriate Sort Complexity
description: Verify sorting uses an O(n log n) algorithm rather than an O(n^2) algorithm for large inputs
test_code: arr.sort()  # Timsort, O(n log n)

name: Avoid Unnecessary Data Copies
description: Verify large collections are not copied when a view/reference would suffice
test_code:
def read_only_view(data):
    return data  # no copy needed for read-only access

name: Avoid Repeated Expensive Calls in Loop Condition
description: Verify expensive calls in a loop condition are hoisted out when the value doesn't change

name: Early Loop Termination When Possible
description: Verify loops searching for a condition exit early via break/return instead of scanning fully

name: Descriptive Variable Naming
description: Verify variable and function names describe their purpose rather than being single letters (except loop indices)
test_code: user_count = len(active_users)

name: No Unexplained Magic Numbers
description: Verify numeric literals with non-obvious meaning are extracted into named constants

name: Bounded Function Length
description: Verify functions remain reasonably short and focused on a single responsibility

name: Single Responsibility per Function
description: Verify a function performs one coherent task rather than several unrelated ones
test_code:
def compute_total(items):
    return sum(item.price for item in items)

name: Public Function Docstring Present
description: Verify public functions include a docstring describing behavior, parameters, and return value

name: Consistent Indentation
description: Verify code uses consistent indentation (spaces vs tabs) throughout a file
test_code:
def f():
    return 1

name: No Resource Leak
description: Verify opened files, sockets, or connections are closed via context managers or explicit cleanup
test_code:
with open(path) as f:
    data = f.read()
